meaning: now that / since (having decided, one must follow through — strong commitment)

exercises.example_sentence: 引き受けたからには、最後まで頑張ります。
exercises.translation: Now that I have accepted, I will do my best to the end.
wrong_choice_explanations: This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜からには' (now that / since (having decided, one must follow through — strong commitment)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜からには' (now that / since (having decided, one must follow through — strong commitment)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜からには' (now that / since (having decided, one must follow through — strong commitment)) needed in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: 挑戦することに決めたからには、成功させます。
exercises.translation: Now that I have decided to challenge, I will make it succeed.
wrong_choice_explanations: た以上は has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where からには is required.
たからといって has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where からには is required.
たことだから has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where からには is required.

exercises.example_sentence: 約束したからには、守らなければならない。
exercises.translation: Now that I have promised, I must keep it.
wrong_choice_explanations: た以上は has a different grammatical function, but 〜からには is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
たからといって has a different grammatical function, but 〜からには is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
たことだから has a different grammatical function, but 〜からには is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.

exercises.example_sentence: 始めたからには、完成させるつもりだ。
exercises.translation: Now that I have started, I intend to complete it.
wrong_choice_explanations: た以上は has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
たからといって has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
たことだから has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.

exercises.example_sentence: 入社したからには、全力で仕事をする。
exercises.translation: Now that I have joined, I will give my all to my work.
wrong_choice_explanations: 〜た以上は is similar; slightly different nuance.
〜たからといって introduces caution; doesn't convey commitment.
〜たことだから is colloquial; less emphatic.
